app_description: Regneflyt is a math game that trains you in mental arithmetic.
app_noscript: Regneflyt requires JavaScript to be enabled in your browser.
complete_confirm_message: Do you want to finish the round?
delete_progress_message: Are you sure you want to delete all your stored progress? This cannot be undone.
offline_message: Regneflyt needs internet to load this page right now.
offline_retry: Try again when you are connected.
error_boundary_message: An unexpected error occurred. Try reloading the page.
feedback_action_addition_basic: Practice single-digit combinations until they feel automatic.
feedback_action_addition_carry: Focus on what happens when digits sum to 10 or more. Build small groups (10, 20, etc.) first.
feedback_action_subtraction_basic: Strengthen your fact fluency with no borrowing needed.
feedback_action_subtraction_borrow: When the bottom digit is bigger, practice borrowing from the tens, hundreds, etc.
feedback_action_multiplication_facts_1to: Build automaticity with 1–5 times tables through repeated practice.
feedback_action_multiplication_facts_6to: Master 6–10 times tables — focus on the harder ones (6x7, 6x8, 7x8, etc.).
feedback_action_multiplication_multi_dig: Practice breaking apart multi-digit problems into smaller steps.
feedback_action_division_facts: Strengthen your understanding of which numbers divide evenly.
feedback_action_addition_algebraic: You're given the sum and one addend. Practice problems like 5 + ? = 13 to build fluency with this format.
feedback_action_subtraction_negative: When you subtract a larger number from a smaller one the result is negative. Practice with the number line and signed numbers.
feedback_action_subtraction_algebraic: You're given the result and one number. Practice problems like ? − 8 = 5 or 12 − ? = 7.
feedback_action_multiplication_facts_11t: Extend your times tables to 11–14. 11×n has simple patterns and 12×n builds on 10×n plus 2×n.
feedback_action_multiplication_algebraic: You're given the product and one factor. Practice finding the missing factor, e.g. 7 × ? = 56. This bridges directly to division.
feedback_action_division_large_tables: You're dividing using the 11–14 times tables. Strengthen your knowledge of these tables and their corresponding division facts.
feedback_action_division_algebraic: You have two of three numbers in a division. Practice recognising the link between multiplication and division to find the missing value.
storage_write_error: Progress could not be saved. Storage may be full.
sw_registration_error: Offline support could not be enabled. Try reloading the page.
toast_copy_link_deterministic_success: Setup link with fixed puzzle order copied.
toast_validation_error: Please fix validation errors before starting.
toast_copy_link_validation_error: Please fix validation errors before copying the setup link.
onboarding_intro: Choose a topic, then press Start.
